News.title: Iran war has exposed the depths to which our once all-conquering Royal Navy has been allowed to sink
News.content:
Sinking feeling

BRITAIN’S feeble response to the war on Iran has exposed the depths to which our once all-conquering Royal Navy has been allowed to sink.

Successive Tory Governments cannot escape their share of the blame for shamefully running down our Armed Forces, despite repeated warnings about funding.



But it’s on Labour’s watch that the UK suffered the humiliation yesterday of learning that HMS Dragon is still clad in scaffolding and will take TWO WEEKS to get to Cyprus to defend our RAF base from Iranian strikes.



In 1982 Margaret Thatcher — who unlike Sir Keir Starmer really WAS a towering Winston Churchill-like Prime Minister — famously ordered an entire Taskforce be mustered to defend the Falklands in less than half that time.

Now, even GREECE — hardly a military superpower — will have warships in position to protect RAF Akrotiri before our Type 45 destroyer arrives.

The Ministry of Defence — which has a history of squandering taxpayers’ money — needs to be properly held to account for failing to keep our ships seaworthy.

Likewise the admirals whose job it is to plan for likely conflict.



The Prime Minister, however, is the person ultimately in charge.

He thought Britain could sit this one out and appeal for “de-escalation”, even as Donald Trump assembled his “big beautiful Armada” in the Gulf.

He couldn’t have been more wrong.



Sir Keir’s failure to properly prepare for the US strike and the inevitable Iranian retaliation against British targets will haunt the rest of his premiership.

What this shambles has made clear is that Britain MUST spend more on defence — and fast.

The answer is not yet more tax rises.

As the Office for Budget Responsibility points out, tax rates are already so high that they are in danger of disincentivising work.

Instead, the UK’s astronomical welfare bill — £404billion by 2030 — must be slashed to pay for a massive ramping up of the nation’s defences.



In trying to defend his chaotic handling of the past week, Sir Keir told MPs his focus is on the serious business of protecting lives.

In which case it should be crystal clear what he has to do.

Helli-days

AS with pledges to support pub landlords before hitting them with higher business rates, what ministers say and what they do are often very different things.

Rachel Reeves claims she wants to help families enjoy a getaway this summer.

RECOMMENDED STORIES



But at the same time the Government’s mean-spirited Visitor Levy is making hols in Britain hideously more expensive.

How is that helping with Labour’s promise to ease the cost of living?

News.title: I built Hitler’s bunker after being captured… we only found out what it was for after the War, says captive, 100
News.content:
A FORMER Nazi captive aged 100 has told how he was forced to dig Adolf ­Hitler’s bunker.

Ches Black was taken by lorry to Germany in 1944 as part of the forced labour drive known as Organisation Todt.





Aged 19, he was dispatched by the Reich Chancery in Berlin with hundreds of workers, German soldiers and others to dig a deep hole by hand.

It was not until the end of the war that he learnt it was the site of Hitler’s bunker, where he killed himself in 1945 with new bride Eva Braun as the Allies closed in.

Ches, of Stoke Row, Oxon, said: “We had no idea what it was for.

“We were just digging a hole and didn’t know we were protecting Hitler.

“Only after the war we found out.”

Ches was born Czeslaw Blachucki and during the war lived with his parents in Vilnius — then part of Poland but now Lithuania.

In 1944, he was rounded up by the Nazis, loaded into a cattle wagon with 40 others and taken to Berlin.



He never saw his mother again.

Ches said: “We were forced labour.

“It was sandy and we just had a wheelbarrow and shovels and worked by hand.

“It was quite deep — as a person in the hole you looked up and up.”

Later, Ches was deployed to Italy, where he transported timber.



He escaped the Nazis, joining Polish resistance fighters until the end of the war.

Unable to return to Soviet-run Poland after 1945, he ended up working for 29 years at a car factory in Cowley, Oxford.

He was married to June for 72 years, having four children.

Ches is recalling his experiences as part of a history project, and to keep alive the memory and heritage of Polish soldiers and refugees placed in Oxfordshire resettlement camps after the war.

News.title: The Government WANTS a cover-up on the grooming gangs scandal… here’s how you can STOP that
News.content:
THE crimes committed by Britain’s grooming and rape gangs are the worst in living memory.

For decades, thousands of children across dozens of towns were groomed, trafficked and raped by gangs of men, most of whom were Pakistani Muslims.





Far too many people within the British state turned a blind eye, or actively worked to cover up these crimes, because they feared being called racist or just didn’t believe the victims.

Police forces, local councils and care homes were all complicit in this cover-up.

The victims of the grooming gangs were let down and ignored by the very institutions that were supposed to protect them.

Last year the Government was dragged, kicking and screaming, into holding a full national inquiry into this issue.



After accusing those who were calling for an inquiry of “jumping on a far-right bandwagon” in January, by June even the Prime Minister had been forced to accept that these crimes really did happen, and that they really were ­covered up.

The public deserves to know the whole truth.

For many grooming gang survivors, the announcement of a full national inquiry offered hope that justice might finally be done, and that those who ­perpetrated or covered up these crimes might finally be punished.

But the devil is in the detail.



After initially committing to an inquiry in the summer, the Government dragged its feet for months, refusing to reveal ­anything more about how the inquiry would work, or who would lead it.

In the autumn, a number of survivors resigned from the Government’s “survivor liaison panel”, raising concerns that they were being manipulated into broadening the scope of the review beyond grooming gangs.

And at the end of last year, when the Government finally released the details of the inquiry, it quickly became clear that they just weren’t serious about uncovering the truth.

Victims are now faced with the prospect of another cover-up.

We now know that the inquiry is set to be chaired by ­Baroness Longfield, a ­Labour peer who has long campaigned for “racial inclusion” in our education system.



Given this background, how can we ­possibly expect her to deal head-on with difficult issues around race and religion, or to properly investigate the role that Labour-run councils played in covering up this abuse?

The inquiry will also be bound by its “terms of reference”, which set out what will be investigated, and to what end.

The draft terms produced by the ­Government are nothing short of a disaster.

If adopted, the inquiry would spend no time looking at the role of race or religion in motivating these crimes, would not lead to any prosecutions of those involved in the cover-up, and would have no authority to look at grooming gang cases which took place before the year 2000, despite the fact these crimes have been taking place for many decades.



It wouldn’t cover every local authority with a history of grooming gang cases or explore why many judges gave such soft sentences to grooming gang perpetrators.

It wouldn’t explore links between grooming gangs and organised crime, or even seek to define what a “grooming gang” is in the first place.

Instead of a proper inquiry designed to uncover the truth, the Government is planning to turn this process into a bureaucratic exercise in “lessons learned”.

It is simply not good enough.

But we can force them to change course and expose the full truth about these ­horrors.



The Government is holding a ­consultation on these terms, with a ­deadline for submissions on Friday.

Any member of the public can submit their thoughts, and demand that the ­Government carries out this inquiry ­properly.

If you think that the Government needs to uncover the full truth about the ­grooming gangs, and punish those ­responsible for the cover-up, then there is still time to force them to do so.

Online at groominggangjustice.uk, you can find the full list of criticisms about the Government’s plans, and a list of demands about how the inquiry should be carried out instead.

If you agree, you can put in your details — name, email address and postcode — and the site will fill out the consultation for you.



It only takes a moment, but if enough people do this, we can force the Government to change the terms of reference, and deliver real justice for the victims of the grooming gangs.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

This cover-up has gone on for far too long, and victims have been let down far too many times.

The Government must listen, and uncover the full truth about these horrific crimes.

News.title: Why should hard-working Brits pay a penny to fly home tax-dodging influencers who gloated about ditching UK for Dubai?
News.content:
I LOVE a sunshine break as much as the next Sexy Beast, but beyond burning off a four-hour lay-over in its vulgar airport, I have never been to Dubai.

I think it has something to do with having no desire whatsoever to go to a soulless sandpit that’s hotter than Satan’s BBQ and where every pub resembles the All Bar One in Milton Keynes.



But mainly my reluctance has been down to the absolute state of some of the people who stuff its air-conditioned ­skyscrapers. And I’m not talking about the locals — whoever they may be.

I mean all those nauseating expats, celebs and “influencers” who insist on telling us, via a thousand TikTok reels a day, that life in Dubai is sooo much ­better than in boring Britain.

Smug “models” and ­“businessmen” who like to remind us that while we’re stuck in the rain paying TAXES — yuck! — they spend all day on a beach counting their dirhams.



People like Rio Ferdinand and his wife, ex-Towie turn Kate, who buggered off to Dubai for “lifestyle, safety, ­weather and just a new life”, with Rio scoffing that he didn’t want to pay tax in the UK when “things are falling apart, going wrong”.

Rio was not alone in thinking Dubai was “safe”.

Many others boasted about that, too, like Luisa Zissman from The ­Apprentice. When she moved there she told how she was “looking forward to not being cold and being able to walk alone at night safely”.

Even Amir Khan — who with several world champion boxing belts under his, er, belt, should be able to ­handle the mean streets of Britain — said: “It’s just so much safer here.”

So how’s that going, guys?



Because it looks to me like it’s Dubai that is unsafe and falling apart now — ­literally.

Thousands are trying to flee this sun-drenched citadel in the United Arab Emirates as well as the various other scorched-earth states in the region, as Tehran lobs missiles in its ­direction.

Rio has now admitted he’s “scared”, while wife Kate is apparently begging him to get out. Alas, they won’t be able to jump on a private jet, with the airports largely closed.

Of course, not every British expat will have such a luxury escape route at their ­disposal, so they will be relying on Britain to help get them out of Dodge.

And by Britain, I mean us mugs, the British tax- payers.



Look, I obviously want all these people to be out of harm’s way ­ — but these self-satisfied tax exiles should stump up for it out of their own silk-lined pockets. Why should we pay a single penny to help?

I certainly don’t blame them for wanting to flee. The mad mullahs of Iran will be ­absolutely loving bombing all these ­hideous “seven-star” hotels full of ­alcohol-swigging infidels.

The more bombs drop on Tehran, the more Tehran will set its sights on ­anything suitably “Western” that its ­missiles can reach.

But you know what? That’s the thing with this part of the world.



It exists in a permanently fragile state — a constant powder keg that has been that way since long before any of us were born.

The Middle East and conflict go together like Love Island and silicone tits.

Sure, the temperature has increased somewhat in recent days — to put it mildly — but it was ever thus.

Wars are constantly erupting in ­Lebanon, Israel, Kuwait, Syria, Iraq, Yemen, Gaza and, once again, Iran.



Now it’s the UAE’s turn to be caught up in all this never-ending aggression (the Emirates was hit by as many ­missiles as Israel in the first 24 hours of this war).

Did all those clever expats really not think about this before they live-streamed their departure to their new “safe” life in the sun?

If not, where the hell did they think they were going?

Because the reality is that whatever European enclave you might chisel out, you are still living smack bang in the most dangerous and intolerant region in the world.

An Instagram utopia where religion trumps everything, and the neighbours build nuclear bombs.



ZACK'S ZERO CHANCE YIKES! Those Green Party loons are polling second place now after their win in Gorton and Denton. People must be smoking a hell of a lot of those illegal substances that Crack Polanski wants to legalise if they really think this ragbag of chancers are the future of Britain. I guess we can hardly blame folk for wondering what else there is to vote for as Labour screws up the country and those on the Right squabble like football dads on a Sunday. But I won’t be rushing to the bookies to put a tenner on PM Polanski any time soon. I think the Greens are having a “Reform moment”, drawing in the fed-up crowds who just want ABS (Anyone But Starmer). And as Nigel Farage is finding out, once you start to put some meat on the bones of your headline-grabbing schtick, it all starts to unravel.

Oi BBC, gizza job. Go on, I can WHO that

DUSTED off my CV this week to apply for the long-vacant position of The Doctor in BBC propaganda tool ­Doctor Who.

As a walking ­compendium of nonsensical one-liners, with a wardrobe bursting with grubby trench coats and wacky ties, I think I’ve got this one in the bag.

I can’t see anything getting in the way . . . except perhaps my age, skin colour, gender, sexuality and political outlook. Wish me luck!

MOSSAD RIDDLE SPIES at Israeli intelligence agency Mossad have apparently been hacking into Tehran’s traffic cameras for years – so much so that they claim they “knew Tehran like we knew Jerusalem”. Impressive stuff, and evidently the key to wiping out Iran’s No1 nutjob, “supreme” leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Good riddance to a very nasty bastard. But I can’t help wondering if US/Israeli intelligence have been putting all their eggs into one basket here. How else to explain why, with all their James Bond gear, they claim to have no knowledge of a missile that slammed into a girls’ school on the day the war started, ­killing, Iranian officials claim, 168 people?

HE’S CRUZ-ING FOR A WELSH BRUISING



YOU would have thought that with all their globe- trotting, Posh and Becks’ kids would know a little geography.

One minute they’re in Paris watching their mum dress catwalk ­models in clothes no one will ever buy, the next they’re eating a pizza on the Amalfi Coast alongside an Irish MMA fighter ­convicted of assault.

Yet somehow young Cruz Beckham – the singer John ­Lennon always dreamed he’d be – doesn’t seem to know which country Cardiff is in. A “souvenir ticket” for his Cruz Beckham And The Breakers gig, at Clwb Ifor Bach in the Welsh capital on ­Friday, had its ­location printed as “Cardiff, England, UK”.

A proper Goldenballs-up, this would have been bad enough at any venue in the city. But it is particularly insulting to one that bills itself as a “proud . . . member’s club for Welsh ­language speakers”.



He’s lucky he got out alive.

KELLY'S SCARED US ALL KELLY OSBOURNE drew a collective gasp with her appearance at the Brits the other night, and you didn’t need to be a cold-hearted troll to have sucked in some air. It is a fact that Ozzy Osbourne’s once- cherubic daughter now looks worryingly thin and fans are understandably concerned about her. Yes, some keyboard cretins have posted nasty things on social media – like the snarky critic who recently said she looked like “a dead body”. But not every comment about a person’s ­appearance is a ­“dehumanising” body-shaming remark, secretly brimming with bile. Many fans are ­genuinely concerned and they should be allowed to express that. I get that Kelly is ­grieving her dad. I lost my mother at Christmas so I know exactly how painful this period is (losing your appetite is just one of many unwanted gifts the Grim Reaper leaves us). I also know how ­important it is to protect yourself from anything that may upset you while you’re not feeling your best. Which would probably include not appearing on a major live TV show that is typically watched by millions of bitchy deadbeats with social media accounts.

LILY ALLEN has annoyed fans by only performing her dreary new album at recent gigs. But I’d say that was a smart move.

If she played any of her old bangers alongside the mostly melody-free songs on West End Girl, her new stuff would be shown to be even more underwhelming than it already is.

News.title: Blackpool Pleasure Beach adds fee for disabled people's carers – 'Unaffordable'
News.content:
Blackpool Pleasure Beach adds fee for disabled people's carers – 'Unaffordable'

Blackpool Pleasure Beach has announced a new charge for carers of disabled people just days before it is set to open, in a move it says is 'in line with the wider attractions industry'

View 2 Images Blackpool Pleasure Beach will open on Saturday for the 2026 season (Image: Blackpool Pleasure Beach)

A major UK theme park has axed free entry for carers of disabled people - a move that one mum says makes the attraction unaffordable.

‌



Blackpool Pleasure Beach will open on Saturday for the 2026 season, and says it has updated its accessibility policy "in line with the wider attractions industry".

‌



There will now be a new charge of £25 for 'essential companions'. These tickets are available "for guests who hold a valid Access Card or Nimbus ID with the +1 symbol".

‌



However, one mum says the new charge makes trips to Blackpool Pleasure Beach "unaffordable". Her son got a pass to the park for Christmas, under the assumption that a family member would be able to accompany him regularly.

View 2 Images There will now be a new charge of £25 for 'essential companions' at Blackpool Pleasure Beach (Image: Getty Images)

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

‌



She told LancsLive: "Blackpool Pleasure Beach have... implemented a charge of £25 per visit for carers accompanying disabled visitors. This was free.

"Many disabled people have already purchased annual passes or tickets, and they have to request the 'companion ticket' close to their visit, and will now be charged.

"And they have announced this three days before the park opens for the season, with many disabled people already having purchased passes or tickets and will now be unable to afford to use them... My autistic son got a pass for Christmas, on the basis that a family member could take him regularly."

‌



A spokesperson from Pleasure Beach Resort said: "For the 2026 season, we are offering Essential Companion tickets at a reasonably adjusted concessionary rate, which is less than half the price of our full-priced ticket, and cheaper than any promotional or online rate that includes riding on any day of the year.

"Essential Companions can continue to be added to our EasyPass scheme free of charge. The EasyPass scheme allows qualifying guests to avoid the physical queue and results in easier ride access. Our eligibility criteria for our accessibility policy has not changed.

"We have made this change to Essential Companions in line with the wider attractions industry, and have worked closely with Nimbus Disability, an organisation founded and run by disabled people for disabled people, on making sure this change is reasonable and fair.

Article continues below

"Any bookings made before March 2 are not affected by this change."

News.title: Cuba in mass blackout including Havana as millions in darkness while oils runs out
News.content:
Cuba in mass blackout including Havana as millions in darkness while oils runs out

Millions of people have been left without power after a major outage struck today.

The western half of Cuba was left in darkness after power cuts hit the island nation, leaving millions of people in Havana and beyond without power as the country struggles with dwindling oil reserves.

The Ministry of Energy and Mines said: “A disconnection has occurred in the National Electric System, SEN,” adding that the blackout affected people from the eastern town of Pinar del Rio to the central town of Camaguey.

Meanwhile Cuban energy minister Vicente de la O Levy said: “We are working on the restoration of the SEN amid a complex energy situation." Crews are working to restore power.

Image: Anadolu via Getty Images) Anadolu via Getty Images)

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

Meanwhile Cuban energy minister Vicente de la O Levy said: “We are working on the restoration of the SEN amid a complex energy situation." Crews are working to restore power.

It is the second such outage to affect Cuba's western region in the past three months. A huge power cut affected the nation after the national grid collapsed in December.

The "unexpected" outage followed a fault at the Antonio Guiteras Thermoelectric Plant, located in Matanzas. It wasn't immediately clear what caused the most recent outage on Wednesday.

Cuba is struggling with dwindling oil reserves after the US attacked Venezuela in early January, a move that halted critical petroleum shipments from the South American country. Later that month, US President Donald Trump threatened to impose tariffs on any country that would sell or supply Cuba with oil.

Cuba gets its power from large thermoelectric plants like Antonio Guiteras and some smaller ones, which require crude oil to operate. The country produces about half of the crude needed, but has to buy the rest from other countries, which can be difficult and costly due to US sanctions. It has also depended on allies like Venezuela and Russia for cheaper fuel.

Last month, Cuba's government implemented austere fuel-saving measures and warned that jet fuel wouldn't be available at nine airports across the island until mid-March.

Prior to the Venezuela attack, the island already was struggling with a crumbling electric grid, generation deficits and interruptions in fuel supplies.

News.title: Firm admits supplying water unfit for human consumption after parasite outbreak
News.content:
Firm admits supplying water unfit for human consumption after parasite outbreak

South West Water Ltd pleaded guilty to supplying contaminated water to customers in Devon in 2024, leaving hundreds of people sick with some requiring hospital treatment and 17,000 homes issued with a 'boil water' notice

View 5 Images South West Water has admitted to supplying parasite-ridden water that left hundreds sick in Devon (Image: Getty Images)

A water company has admitted to supplying parasite-contiminated water to its customers homes in what an MP has branded an "awful event" that left some people hospitalised and hundreds sick with vomiting and diarrhoea.

‌



South West Water Ltd pleaded guilty to an offence under s70(1) of the Water Industry Act 1991 to supplying water unfit for human consumption during a hearing at Exeter Magistrates' Court.

‌



The firm was being prosecuted by the Drinking Water Inspectorate (DWI) following a 2024 incident in and around Brixham, Devon, that hospitalised four and led to more than 140 confirmed cases of people infected with the cryptosporidium parasite.

‌



About 17,000 households and businesses in the area were issued with a "boil water" notice, some lasting as long as eight weeks, as case reports emerged, and the firm now faces a fine following the "extremely high-profile" incident.

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

View 5 Images People were infected with parasites following a contamination incident around Brixham, Devon (Image: PA)

‌



Howard Leithead, representing the DWI, had asked for the case to be sent to the crown court for sentencing as it is "high-profile or exceptionally sensitive".

He said: "This was an extremely high-profile incident that occurred in 2024. Not only was this very high-profile, but this incident affected a significant number of people across a broad geographical area.

"It resulted in an adverse impact on the public confidence in the water supply in this area. Its effects were, some of the complaints say, long felt after the lifting of the boil water notices.

‌



"There was, of course, local inconvenience, economic impact, and impact on education throughout this incident. Given the high-profile nature of this case, and to ensure that the message is heard loud and clear by local residents that the criminal justice system will treat offences of this nature with the utmost seriousness, we respectfully submit that the crown court would be the most appropriate venue to deal with this case, given the factors identified in our note."

View 5 Images The Cryptosporidium parasite causes gastrointestinal distress, such as vomiting and diarrhoea (Image: Getty Images/iStockphoto)

Dominic Kay KC, representing South West Water, said the firm had pleaded guilty at the first instance and had submitted a basis of plea. He said the case could be sentenced by a district judge in the magistrates' court.

‌



He said: "We would respectfully submit that it isn't a complex case, and certainly not one of such complexity that this court cannot deal with. Factually there is not a huge amount between the parties, as I understand, matters. The only real bone of contention, as the court has rightly observed, is often the case in these prosecutions, where the case sits on culpability.

"This is precisely the sort of case that district judges should, and we say, do deal with on a very regular basis, and it's not simply in relation to environmental cases."

View 5 Images South West Water owner Pennon previously said the final bill for the outbreak reached nearly £40 million (Image: Getty Images)

‌



District Judge Stuart Smith rejected the prosecution submission and said he would retain jurisdiction. South West Water owner Pennon previously said the final bill for the outbreak reached nearly £40 million.

The supplier - which also owns Bristol Water, Bournemouth Water and SES Water - said it was facing costs of around £36 million for the supply contamination incident and its "reshaping and transformation programmes".

Caroline Voaden, Liberal Democrat MP for South Devon, said after the hearing: "This time last year, the former South West Water chief executive claimed customers were among the company's top priorities.

‌



"Today, they have pleaded guilty to supplying water unfit for human consumption. This admission of guilt has been a long time coming. I am glad that SWW have owned up to their serious failures. This awful event should never have happened. But the mismatch between rhetoric and action plagues our broken water industry."

View 5 Images Marcus Rink, chief inspector of the Drinking Water Inspectorate, said the company's decision to plead guilty 'reflects the seriousness of the failings identified during our investigation' (Image: PA)

"Whether it is protecting customers or the environment, too many water firms say one thing, then do another. I want to thank the Drinking Water Inspectorate for bringing the case and the many campaigners who worked tirelessly to ensure the anguish they felt during the cryptosporidium outbreak was not forgotten.

Article continues below

"Last summer, the Government finally started to overhaul our water industry by promising to abolish Ofwat. But progress has been glacially slow. They must go much further and faster - this industry must be properly regulated and held to account."

Marcus Rink, chief inspector of the Drinking Water Inspectorate, said: "The company's decision to plead guilty to the offences relating to the Brixham incident reflects the seriousness of the failings identified during our investigation. While such incidents are very rare, this incident had a significant impact on the public and the wider community."

Water minister Emma Hardy added: "Contamination of drinking water is rare but it is utterly unacceptable. The communities affected by this abhorrent incident in Brixham deserve answers and today's guilty plea is a crucial step toward accountability."

News.title: Snow maps reveal shock twin blizzards to bury UK and hit eight major cities
News.content:
Snow maps reveal shock twin blizzards to bury UK and hit eight major cities

Brits could see two blizzards bringing snow before the end of March as weather forecasting maps reveal as many as eight major cities will see some of the white stuff

View 6 Images Snow and rain is on the cards on March 13 (Image: WX CHARTS)

Despite temperatures rising across the UK this week, weather maps now show snow is on the horizon.

‌



Shocking new weather maps reveal two blizzards could sweep across England, Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland before the end of this month, bringing flurries to as many as eight cities.

‌



The GFS weather model first shows snow and rain moving across the UK from the Atlantic on March 13, with heavy snow falling in Northern Ireland and Scotland while fierce rain hits Wales and England. By midday, maps show some snow also falling in North Wales and northern parts of England. Torrential rain is expected in London around this time.

‌



View 6 Images Snow (in purple) falling at midday on March 13 (Image: WX CHARTS)

UK snow maps reveal storm bringing 11 inches is days away from burying cities READ MORE :

Snow maps reveal -4C blizzard will bring flurries to major UK cities next week READ MORE :

Snow coverage maps for the morning of March 14 show snow settled on the ground around Gloucester, the far north-west of England, almost all of Northern Ireland including Belfast, and a massive chunk of Scotland including Glasgow.

However, a wider storm is expected to follow on March 15, with maps showing snow falling over Scotland, northern England and Wales at around 9am. By midday, the maps show snow will have reached the Midlands too.

‌



View 6 Images Snow coverage (in purple) on March 14 (Image: WX CHARTS)

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

Snow coverage maps for 6pm on March 15 show more cities including Stoke-on-Trent, Manchester, Edinburgh, Dundee and Aberdeen could see the white stuff settled on the ground.

‌



BBC Weather expects temperatures to remain above average for this period. However, BBC forecasters say there could also be one or two chilly interludes.

View 6 Images More snow and rain is expected on March 15 (Image: WX CHARTS)

The BBC forecast for March 9 to 15 states: "Next week it looks like Atlantic systems will become more active. These will bring occasional rain and brisk winds, although aimed more at northern and western areas.

‌



"Frontal systems will perhaps sometimes struggle to cross the southern and eastern UK as European high pressure continues to exert an influence. As a result, the south and east could have near to below-average precipitation amounts, while northern and western regions are wetter than normal, especially Northern Ireland and western Scotland.

View 6 Images Several major cities could see snow before the end of March (Image: WX CHARTS)

"With the expected pattern often drawing in south-east to south-westerly flows, mild conditions are expected overall, with temperatures on average above what is expected in March. However, in between frontal systems there will be transient high pressure ridges which could bring the chance of one or two chilly and perhaps frosty or foggy nights."

‌



The Met Office says for March 9 to 18: "The first part of this period will continue to see the UK located at the interface between high pressure across Europe and low pressure over the North Atlantic.

View 6 Images Snow coverage (in purple) on March 15 (Image: WX CHARTS)

"As such, western areas will likely see spells of rain at times, along with stronger winds, whereas eastern parts should see more in the way of drier interludes, though even here, some rain is possible from time to time.

Article continues below

"Towards the middle of March, there is still a signal for unsettled conditions to become rather more widespread, with all parts seeing a higher chance of rain and stronger winds. Throughout, temperatures should overall pan out a little above average for most areas."

News.title: Major update after Trump ‘ended all trade to Spain’ with threat to seize bases
News.content:
The Trump administration has issued an update after the US president threatened to cut off trade with a major European power.

White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt told reporters that Spain has "agreed to cooperate with the US military". It comes after Trump threatened to impose a full trade embargo on Spain after the NATO ally refused to allow American forces to use its bases at Morón and Rotafor for operations linked to strikes on Iran.

"With respect to Spain, I think they heard the president’s message yesterday loud and clear," Leavitt said. "And it’s my understanding over the past several hours, they’ve agreed to cooperate with the US military. And so I know that the US military is coordinating with their counterparts in Spain."

Image: La Moncloa) La Moncloa)

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

In a meeting with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz on Tuesday, Trump said: "We're going to cut off all trade with Spain. We don't want anything to do with Spain."

He also accused Spain of being a "terrible partner" in NATO for failing to increase its defence budget in line with a target of five per cent of its GDP.

"It started when every European nation, at my request, made five per cent, which they should be doing. Everybody was enthusiastic - Germany, everybody - but Spain didn't do it," he said.

"And now," he continued, "Spain actually said we can’t use their bases. And that’s alright we can use their base if we want. We can just fly in and use it. Nobody’s going to tell us not to use it."

Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez hit back at Trump's remarks this morning, reaffirming his opposition to war and what he called the "breakdown of international law".

In a 10-minute televised address, the Spanish prime minister reflected on the wars in Ukraine and Gaza, as well as the Iraq War in 2003, and said the Spanish government's stance could be summed up as "no to war".

Image: Getty Images) Getty Images)

Foreign Minister José Manuel Albares has categorically denied Leavitt's claim that Spain will "cooperate military" with the US, according to El País.

"The Spanish government's position on the war in the Middle East, the bombings in Iran, and the use of our bases has not changed one iota," Albares said in an interview with Cadena SER radio.

"Our position of 'no to war' remains clear and unequivocal," he said, adding that he has "no idea" what Leavitt was referring to. "There is an agreement, a bilateral accord, and outside the framework of that bilateral agreement, there will be no use of Spanish sovereign bases. Any operation must be within the framework of the United Nations."

In response to Trump's threats, the Élysée Palace earlier said French President Emmanuel Macron had conveyed his "solidarity" with Spain during a phone call with Sánchez today.

EU internal market commissioner Stéphane Séjourné also expressed his solidarity with Spain, saying earlier today: "Any threat against a member state is by definition a threat against the EU.

"I want to be very clear here, from this point of view, the EU’s competency on trade is actually dealt with by the Commission. If you threaten one particular country… well, that’s we’ve seen about Greenland. I think we saw that there was a lot of unity."

News.title: Billy Idol overdosed on heroin before Top Of The Pops then came huge turning point
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: Billy Idol overdosed on heroin before Top Of The Pops then came huge turning point

As a new documentary sheds light on Billy Idol's incredible life, the legendary rocker tells of the drugs that threatened to "ruin" his career - and the time he came close to death

View 9 Images Rock legend Billy Idol (Image: Getty Images)

White Wedding was an anthem for the MTV generation of the 1980s when, with his peroxide hair and snarling attitude, Billy Idol epitomised the sex, drugs and rock 'n' roll punk dream. Now a new documentary about the hell-raising rocker reveals how he almost dropped dead after snorting heroin days before launching his solo career in the UK.

‌



Billy had just conquered America with his breakthrough 1983 album Rebel Yell, when he almost blew his Top of the Pops debut. He says: "I did OD and nearly died. I went to America in March 1981 and came back to the UK in triumph in 1984, after Rebel Yell.

‌



“I was due on Top of the Pops. I nearly ruined it and nobody knew. On our return to London we met some pals and they had some of the strongest heroin, Persian Brown, I think. Everybody did a line and they all nodded out except me and a mate and we did a few more lines. I was basically dying. I was turning blue. So they put me in an ice bath and walked me around on the roof of the building."

‌



Billy, now clean aged 70, relives the madness of his past in his new two hour documentary, Billy Idol Should be Dead, which premieres on Sky Arts later this month. Chronicling his anarchic beginnings in the London punk scene, before finding superstardom in the MTV era, when White Wedding and Rebel Yell ruled the charts, the film reveals the near-deadly consequences of his battle with drugs.

9 View 9 Images Billy in 1990 (Image: Getty Images) 9 View 9 Images Billy in 1979 (Image: Popperfoto via Getty Images)

‌



Featuring his late mother Joan, longtime girlfriend Perri Lister and his three children, the film also tells how a freak DNA test led to Billy discovering his love child son Brant. He says: "There are elements of my life that I probably should not have survived. I am still here and I am still rocking and in love with what I do.”

Of the punk rock era, he says: "The drugs just happened to come along. Drugs ruined some things, but life is just a little like that." Inspired by The Sex Pistols while growing up in Bromley, south London, as a geeky bespectacled teenager, christened William Broad, he was the son of middle class parents William and Joan.

Billy says: "When I was 14, it was when the music scene exploded. My dad did not understand me, as I was not trying at school. We all wanted to be David Bowie. In the 70s we were just told there was no future as there were no jobs. I saw The Pistols and that's what we were waiting for."

‌



Forming Generation X, Billy toured the London scene, but moved to New York when the punk dream ended. He says: "When I arrived in New York all I had was a mattress, some sheets and a guitar. “The record company gave me money and I would buy some pot and weak smack [heroin]. I would go out to dance clubs."

9 View 9 Images Rock singer Billy Idol and his father and mother pose for a portrait at the Minneapolis Auditorium in Minneapolis, Minnesota on April 28, 1987 (Image: Getty Images) 9 View 9 Images Billy Idol of Generation X performs on stage, London, 1978 (Image: Redferns)

‌



In the Manhattan clubs, Billy was introduced to hard drugs. He says: "A lot of the people we loved were heroin addicts back then. Lou Reed wrote the song Heroin. We were not thinking how dangerous it was. Instead you think ‘maybe this can unleash something’”.

Becoming close pals with Chic star Nile Rodgers, he recalls on the documentary an infamous night when Billy threw up over his idol David Bowie, who was sitting at the back of a club. Nile says: “ Billy went ‘bloody hell, that's David f*cking Bowie’. And as he said ‘Bowie’ he barfed [vomited.] And then he went to Bowie ‘Hello mate!’”

But the fun transcended into despair for Billy, as his drug addiction deepened. Eventually, his dad flew to New York to rescue him. Billy says: "Dad came off the plane with a big stick. I said ‘why have you brought that?’ And he said ‘I'm going to beat you with it’. He was here to take command of everything. I needed him to come to wake me up a little bit, as I did need somebody to show they cared."

‌



Sadly, Billy's recovery didn’t last, as he got sucked back into the New York scene, where drugs were on every corner. As his stardom grew, with the advent of MTV, Billy cleaned up for a while. And he moved to Los Angeles with his girlfriend of nine years, former Hot Gossip dancer, Perri Lister, saying: "I started to realise it [drug addiction] was changing my personality and was perverting me in a way. But it takes a lot for the real personality to take back control. It takes a lot more than you think. That's the trouble. In LA we were living in the daylight, but in New York we were living a vampire existence."

View 9 Images Billy Idol performs last year (Image: Getty Images)

Unfortunately, in February 1990, he was involved in a serious crash while riding his Harley Davidson through Hollywood, and nearly had to have his right leg amputated. He says: "I was on morphine. Even the pain doctor said I was drinking it in. My leg used to look like my brain was coming out. For a while I could look into it and see my bone. They put a metal rod into it."

‌



Thankfully, Billy got back on his feet, but his drug addiction took hold again when his relationship with Peri ended in 1989. He says: "I went off to Thailand with my roadie. We were not going to take drugs, but after a week we asked a cab driver if he could get us some coke. He brought back this long thin vial of this white stuff. It was so strong it was probably China White [opioid].

"You only needed a pin prick and you were as high as a kite. I looked like a ghost. I had some film of us and I destroyed the tape because it was too disturbing. We were out of our minds. I passed out in the elevator [of a hotel] and Mel Gibson was there on holiday with his family, horrified."

Things got worse, as he tried to come down from his drug-induced high. He recalls: “I picked up this large log and threw it through a glass window. A guy threatened to call the police, but we said we'd pay the $20,000 damages. The silver lining was I did finally put heroin behind me. The whole experience was horrible and it was a turning point, as I did not like who I was.'

‌



9 View 9 Images Billi and Perri (Image: Ron Galella Collection via Getty Images) 9 View 9 Images British punk singer and songwriter Billy Idol and his partner, actress, dancer, and singer Perri Lister (Image: Sygma via Getty Images)

Clean and still touring, Billy is a very different man today and loves being a dad to his son Willem, 37, by his ex Perri Lister; and daughter Bonnie, 36, with former flame Linda Mathis. He is also getting closer to his 40-year-old son Brant, his love child, conceived on his 1985 Rebel Yell tour, who he only discovered in 2023 after his daughter took a DNA test, revealing the half sibling she never knew.

‌



Billy says Brant has given him a new lease of life, as well as two grandchildren he never knew he had. He says: "It has all drawn us very much together. This ended up being more important to me than being Billy Idol. I now see why you have children - for the grandchildren."

* Billy Idol Should be Dead premieres on Sky Arts and streams on NOW on March 26

Wild parties were too much for Billy's ex Perri Lister

Billy's ex-girlfriend Perri Lister recalls his wild parties with groupies, during their nine year relationship in the 1980s. She says: “ I once knocked on his door and there were two naked girls there. He was so furious that he got caught. He slammed the door and called security on me! They escorted me out of the hotel and when I got back to our apartment they were changing the locks on me!”

‌



View 9 Images Billy Idol and Perri Lister at Perri Lister's birthday party at the Cat Club. April 10, 1984 (Image: Paul Bruinooge/Patrick McMullan via Getty Images)

Perri, a dancer with Hot Gossip, who regularly appeared on The Kenny Everett Show, says worse was to come when Billy’s drug dealer moved in next door. Mum to his son Willem, she says: “The guy would just bang on the wall and he [the dealer] would come. He [Billy] was cooking up coke in saucepans and it was exploding all over the place.” Perri, who appeared in the White Wedding video and now lives in Bali, also recalls overhearing Billy calling his mistress through the baby monitor at their LA home.

She was beside the pool when Billy said he was going inside to call his roadie, but used the phone near the baby monitor beside the crib where Willem was sleeping. She recalls: “I hear the whole conversation where he says ‘yeah hi baby, it is OK. She [Perri] is going for lunch with her mother. I will pick you up about 1.30pm.’ So he comes out and I said ‘you lying mother f*cker.’ For Perri, it was the final straw.

Article continues below

*If you or someone else needs urgent help after taking drugs or drinking, call 999 for an ambulance. To find drug support advice near you, visit talktofrank.com/get-help/find-support-near-you.
